business.headline: Bangladesh hikes jet fuel prices by nearly 80% amid global energy crisisThe Bangladesh Energy Regulatory Commission (BERC) on Tuesday increased jet fuel prices by nearly 80 percent, marking the second revision this month. The move comes as the ripple effect of rising global fuel rates, as tensions in key oil-producing regions continue to disrupt market stability.
Reliance buys 5 million barrels of Iranian oil after US waiver, sources sayThe development comes after the US administration announced a 30-day waiver on certain sanctions related to Iranian oil.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has suggested that India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Only 40% women invest beyond FDs and gold: Why the gap still exists?Indian women are known for their strong saving habits, but when it comes to investing beyond fixed deposits and gold, many still hold back. What is stopping them from taking that next step?
Markets bounce back, Sensex up 1,300 points, banks leadThe Sensex surged 1,372 points to close at 74,068.45, while the Nifty 50 rose 399.75 points to settle at 22,912.40, reclaiming the 22,900 mark.

Markets in panic mode, gold and silver also hit: What's left for investors?Since the Iran war began, the Sensex has fallen about 10.6%, while the Nifty has dropped nearly 11%. At the same time, gold and silver have also declined, leaving investors with limited places to hide.

Form 16 gone from April 2026: What will your employer give you instead?A key document for salaried taxpayers is set to change. Starting April 2026, Form 16 may no longer be issued, with a new format stepping in to reshape how your income details are reported.

Goldman Sachs cuts India growth forecast to 5.9%, flags rate hike risksThe revision follows changes in Goldman's assumptions on oil prices and supply disruptions. Higher crude prices remain a major risk for India, which relies heavily on imports.

8th Pay Commission: When will your salary rise and how much can you getCentral government employees are eagerly waiting for the 8th Pay Commission update. While a decent salary hike is expected, the bigger question remains, i.e., when will the revised pay actually start coming in?

Swiggy hikes platform fee to Rs 17.58 after Zomato, food orders get costlierFood delivery bills are inching up again. Swiggy has increased its platform fee soon after Zomato, meaning customers will now pay more each time they order online.

Bought gold during Iran war? Here's why it's not giving you returnsGold and silver prices have fallen despite ongoing war tensions, which is unusual as precious metals are typically seen as a safe investment during crises like war.

Kotak Mahindra Bank shares rise: Why is the stock gaining today?The sharp rise reflects a recovery after recent weakness, but investors should watch for consistency in momentum as broader market trends and foreign investor flows continue to influence banking stocks.

Gold falls Rs 2,500, silver down Rs 8,000: Why are safe-haven assets tumbling?Gold and silver prices are falling even as the West Asia conflict continues. Here's why safe-haven assets are losing appeal and what it means for investors.

Sensex rises 1,700 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Sensex and Nifty rebounded sharply on Tuesday as tensions eased, oil prices fell and investors returned to banking stocks after Monday's sharp fall.
Sensex, Nifty jump as US delays Iran grid strike; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 993.75 points to 73,690.14, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 319.20 points to 22,831.85 as of 9:29 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets crash again amid Iran war?Early signals show a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,878 at around 8:40 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open about 1.6% higher compared to Monday's close of 22,512.65.

$580 million bets before Trump's Iran remark. Coincidence or insider trading?Perfectly timed oil and equity trades worth $580 million before US President Donald Trump claimed "productive talks" with Iran and paused strikes, later denied by Tehran, are raising insider trading concerns.

IndiGo appoints former Air India Express CEO Aloke Singh as Strategy ChiefBetween December 3 and 5, 2025, IndiGo cancelled 2,507 flights and delayed 1,852 others, affecting over three lakh passengers nationwide. The disruptions were linked to challenges in implementing revised Flight Duty Time Limitation (FDTL) norms for pilots.

Wall Street rallies as Trump delays Iran strikesThe positive sentiment followed Trump's statement that the US would postpone planned strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days.

Sebi eases trade rules for foreign investors amid rising market outflowsUntil now, investors had to settle each buy and sell trade separately. This meant higher funding needs and more complex transactions. With net settlement, they can offset buy and sell positions, reducing the amount of money required for each cycle.

entertainment.headline: Kim Kardashian, Lewis Hamilton walk arm-in-arm in Japan. Watch viral video Kim Kardashian and Lewis Hamilton were seen together in Tokyo, fueling fresh speculation about their relationship. A viral video adds a new twist to the ongoing dating rumours, keeping fans and media abuzz.

Dhurandhar 2's Rizwan writes a note for Ranveer Singh: Will always have your back Dhurandhar 2 continues to dominate the box office, cementing its position as one of the year's biggest hits. Mustafa Ahmed, who plays Rizwan in the film, wrote an emotional note to Ranveer Singh, offering a deeper glimpse into the bond that shapes their on-screen partnership.

Even fake collections don't reach Rs 100 crore: Kunal Kohli on Dhurandhar's success Kunal Kohli praised the box office success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He expressed admiration for Ranveer Singh and Aditya Dhar for showing the true potential of Indian cinema.

R Madhavan on Dhurandhar 2 smoking scene row: No intention to hurt Sikh sentiments R Madhavan responded to the controversy around a Dhurandhar: The Revenge scene, clarifying that there was no disrespect intended towards the Dasam Granth. The actor called it a misunderstanding.

I treat Sara Arjun like my daughter: Dhurandhar's Rakesh Bedi on viral 'kiss' clip Rakesh Bedi addressed the controversy over his interaction with Sara Arjun at the Dhurandhar trailer launch. He called it a misunderstood gesture of affection.

Bridgerton Season 5 ditches Eloise, turns spotlight on Francesca-Michaela's story Bridgerton Season 5 moves away from fans' highly anticipated arc of Eloise's story. The show now follows Francesca and Michaela, opening a new chapter in the series.

Modi's 2014 win, demonetisation, Atiq Ahmed: The real events that fuel Dhurandhar 2 By weaving in real-life flashpoints – from Atiq Ahmed's killing to demonetisation in India and Narendra Modi's 2014 victory speech – Dhurandhar: The Revenge blurs the line between fiction and reality. Aditya Dhar uses recent history to lend urgency, scale, and unsettling authenticity to the gritty narrative.

Koi Mil Gaya meets Project Hail Mary: Hrithik Roshan, Ryan Gosling spark global buzz In a rare crossover, Hrithik Roshan and Ryan Gosling come together for a global conversation on Project Hail Mary. The moment has sparked excitement and raised questions about what comes next.

Kunickaa Sadanand slams viral post linking her to Kumar Sanu: Have some shame Kunickaa Sadanand slams a viral AI-generated post linking her to Kumar Sanu. She called for respect and warning of legal action.

Ram Gopal Varma asks Pakistan to make their own Dhurandhar: The Truth Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma asks Pakistan to make their own version of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. His remarks intensify debate around the film's narrative and reception.

Called Chamundi Daiva a demon: Court asks Ranveer Singh to apologise, visit temple Bollywood star Ranveer Singh is set to apologise in court and visit the Chamundi temple following allegations of mimicking a deity during interaction with Kantara star Rishab Shetty. The Karnataka High Court emphasised the responsibility of public figures to respect religious sentiments.

Is Badshah married? Video of rapper and actor Isha Rikhi goes viral A video and photos showing rapper Badshah and actor Isha Rikhi participating in wedding rituals have sparked widespread speculation about a possible marriage. However, there is no official confirmation from Badshah or his team, leaving the true nature of the event unclear in Indian media circles.

In Arijit Singh's song from Bhooth Bangla, Akshay-Wamiqa's breezy romance. Watch Akshay Kumar and Wamiqa Gabbi's romantic track 'Tu Hi Disda' from Bhooth Bangla offers a soothing, visually rich glimpse into the film.

Dhurandhar 2 actor Manav Gohil says he didn't audition for the film, talks success Manav Gohil reflected on the massive success of Dhurandhar 2, admitting the scale of its box office impact still hasn't sunk in. From a last-minute casting call to sharing screen space with Ranveer Singh and R Madhavan, the actor opened up about his journey.

RGV reacts to Aishwarya Rai's comeback rumours in Sarkar 4, discusses film's scale Ram Gopal Varma teased a bigger cinematic world for his upcoming film Sarkar 4. He also addressed speculations about Aishwarya Rai Bachchan's casting in the film.

Karan Johar hugs Yami Gautam at event, calls her one of his favourite actors At a recent event, Karan Johar and Yami Gautam shared a warm hug. Their camaraderie amid the announcement of Yami's new film Nayyi Navelli have sparked conversations across social media.

India's global pop group era begins: BTS' label HYBE announces nationwide auditions BTS' agency, HYBE, launches India-wide auditions to discover and train the next global pop act, marking a cultural shift in how Indian talent is scouted and developed.

Pics: Priyanka Chopra joins Anne Hathaway, Dua Lipa and Jake Gyllenhaal in Milan Priyanka Chopra attended a high-profile jewellery event in Milan alongside stars like Anne Hathaway and Dua Lipa. She posed for photos and mingled with fellow celebrities at the event.

Understanding Major Iqbal: Why Dhurandhar's chilling villain loses edge by climax Arjun Rampal's Major Iqbal is equal parts menacing and vulnerable. Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge gives him the kind of arc that breaks the usual Bollywood template of a villain, and yet just when it reaches a high, the film does something unthinkable.

general news.headline: Trump dials PM Modi amid Gulf war, discusses Mideast crisis, Strait of HormuzPrime Minister Narendra Modi said he had a "useful exchange" with the US President. In a post on X, PM said India supports de-escalation and early restoration of peace, stressing that keeping the Strait of Hormuz open, secure and accessible is vital for global stability.

Harish Rana, allowed passive euthanasia by Supreme Court, dies after 13 years of painHarish Rana's case marked a landmark moment in India's legal and medical history, with doctors carrying out the Supreme Court-mandated withdrawal of life support under a supervised and dignity-focused protocol.

Kamal Haasan's MNM opts out of 2026 Tamil Nadu polls, backs DMK-led frontKamal Haasan's Makkal Needhi Maiam (MNM) has opted out of the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, citing differences over seat-sharing terms with the DMK-led alliance. The party, however, has pledged unconditional support to the alliance.

IPL champions RCB sold to Aditya Birla Group-led consortium for Rs 16,000 croreIPL franchise, Royal Challengers Bengaluru, has been sold to a consortium, led by Aditya Birla Group, for a record sum of over Rs 16,000 crore, marking the league's most expensive franchise takeover ever.

Why panicked Pakistan is racing to broker a US-Iran truceThe Iran war has entered a sustained and dangerous phase. Amid this, Pakistan has rushed to mediate between the US and Iran and bring an end to the war. It has positioned itself as the lead mediator. Why does Pakistan want to mediate the US-Iran war so badly?

Indian-origin Texas judge convicted of money laundering, faces 10 years in jailA US county judge, who made history as the first Indian-origin judge in Fort Bend, Texas, has been convicted of financial crimes. KP George, who traces his roots to Kerala, was found guilty of money laundering with prosecutors arguing he had misused funds intended for his election campaign.

Form 16 gone from April 2026: What will your employer give you instead?A key document for salaried taxpayers is set to change. Starting April 2026, Form 16 may no longer be issued, with a new format stepping in to reshape how your income details are reported.

Rajasthan Royals sold for Rs 15,000 crore to US-based entrepreneur: ReportA US-led consortium has reportedly acquired Rajasthan Royals for USD 1.63 billion, setting a new benchmark in the Indian Premier League. Meanwhile, bidding for RCB intensifies, with valuations expected to cross USD 2 billion following their historic 2025 title and massive global demand.

Ben Duckett pulls out of IPL 2026, apologises to Delhi Capitals, faces 2-year banIPL 2026: Ben Duckett withdraws from IPL 2026 to focus on England duty, apologises to Delhi Capitals, and could face a ban under league rules for pulling out after being picked at the auction.

Divine spirit spoke? No, it was Siri: How Nashik godman conned believersA teacher from Nashik has accused self-styled godman Ashok Kharat of using technology to stage 'divine messages' and exploit followers. The claim comes as a growing number of women accuse Kharat of sexual abuse and exploitation.
This Indian city is the most polluted in the world. It's not DelhiAll 25 cities in the global bottom tier in terms of pollution were located in India, Pakistan, or China.

R Madhavan on Dhurandhar 2 smoking scene row: No intention to hurt Sikh sentimentsR Madhavan responded to the controversy around a Dhurandhar: The Revenge scene, clarifying that there was no disrespect intended towards the Dasam Granth. The actor called it a misunderstanding.

Why Trump is desperate to end the war and Iran is in no hurryDonald Trump is keen to cut losses and end the Iran war before it becomes even a bigger liability for him and the United States. However, Iran is reluctant to play ball.Its reluctance stems from its experience negotiating with the Trump administration.

After Larijani's killing, Iran appoints Mohammad Zolghadr as security chiefMohammad Baqer Zolqadr was appointed as Larijani's successor as secretary of the SNSC, the Iranian president's deputy of communications posted on X on Tuesday.

Can ED go to cops if CM barges in during raid? Supreme Court asks BengalThe Supreme Court was hearing West Bengal's objections to the maintainability of the ED plea filed under Article 32. The ED has sought a CBI probe into the alleged obstruction of its raids on I-PAC by Mamata Banerjee and her government.

Called Chamundi Daiva a demon: Court asks Ranveer Singh to apologise, visit templeBollywood star Ranveer Singh is set to apologise in court and visit the Chamundi temple following allegations of mimicking a deity during interaction with Kantara star Rishab Shetty. The Karnataka High Court emphasised the responsibility of public figures to respect religious sentiments.

Even fake collections don't reach Rs 100 crore: Kunal Kohli on Dhurandhar's successKunal Kohli praised the box office success of Dhurandhar: The Revenge. He expressed admiration for Ranveer Singh and Aditya Dhar for showing the true potential of Indian cinema.

Live: Trump shares Shehbaz's post on Pakistan's willingness to mediateUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict is in its fourth week with no let-up, even as tentative signs of de-escalation emerge alongside continued military posturing. Iran launched multiple waves of missiles at Israel on Monday, setting off air raid sirens in Tel Aviv and beyond, even as US President Donald Trump delayed a planned strike on Iranian energy infrastructure citing "productive" talks with Tehran. The attacks, which caused damage to homes in northern Israel but no reported deaths, came amid heightened tensions after Iran moved to effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy route. Meanwhile, at a roundtable in Tennessee, Trump suggested Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth was the first to push for military action. Stay with IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand: RTIAn RTI reply has suggested that India's strategic oil reserves cover less than 10 days of demand, even as global supply risks rise amid the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

Girl, 9, dies of rabies months after dog bite; had skipped injection due to fearThe girl's condition worsened suddenly days before her death, prompting hospitalisation, where she died during treatment. Health officials have urged immediate vaccination after animal bites and greater awareness among parents to prevent such fatalities.
The JJ Juggernaut: Peak of 'Puratchi' Politics | Part 36
This country has the most polluted air in the world. It's not IndiaOnly 14% of global cities met the WHO's annual PM2.5 guideline in 2025, down from 17% the year before.

Girls lead by 1.05% in Class 8Rajasthan Board has declared the Class 5 and Class 8 results today, March 24, 2026. Students can check their scores online through the official Shala Darpan portal, the link for which is rajshaladarpan.rajasthan.gov.in. More than 26 lakh students reportedly appeared for the RBSE Class 5 and Class 8 examinations this year.

Dhurandhar 2 box office collection live: Historic Tuesday loading for Ranveer's filmDhurandhar The Revenge live updates: Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge is on a phenomenal run at the box office – both domestic and worldwide. The film is now finishing its Day 6 run on a solid note in India. Stay tuned for the latest figures.

sports.headline: IPL champions RCB sold to Aditya Birla Group-led consortium for Rs 16,000 crore Indian Premier League franchise, Royal Challengers Bengaluru, has been sold to a consortium, led by Aditya Birla Group, for a record Rs 16,732 crore, marking the league's most expensive franchise takeover ever.

Andre Russell emotional after KKR retire his No. 12 jersey in special ceremony IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday retired Andre Russell's No. 12 jersey in honour of his contributions to the team. Russell, who retired from IPL last year, joined the backroom staff for the upcoming season, starting March 28.

When you think of KKR, it's Shah Rukh Khan first and then Rinku Singh: Ajinkya Rahane IPL 2026: Rinku Singh named KKR vice-captain as Rahane and Nayar praised his growth, leadership and impact, with the T20 World Cup winner now taking on a bigger role.

Chess great Koneru Humpy slammed for pulling out of Candidates over security fears Cyprus Chess Federation chief Criton Tornaritis has criticised Koneru Humpy's withdrawal from the Women's Candidates over safety concerns, calling it a bad move that harms the sport and organisers.

KKR announce new vice-captain, Rinku Singh to be Ajinkya Rahane's deputy IPL 2026: Kolkata Knight Riders on Tuesday announced Rinku Singh as their vice-captain for the new season. Ajinkya Rahane will continue to lead the three-time champions, who finished eighth in the 10-team league last season.

RCB vs SRH tickets sell out in a minute, fans allege lack of transparency Tickets for the IPL 2026 opener between RCB and SRH sold out within minutes, leaving fans frustrated, with many alleging lack of transparency and irregularities in the booking process.

Ben Duckett pulls out of IPL 2026, apologises to Delhi Capitals, faces 2-year ban IPL 2026: Ben Duckett withdraws from IPL 2026 to focus on England duty, apologises to Delhi Capitals, and could face a ban under league rules for pulling out after being picked at the auction.

IPL as big as EPL and NBA, says Sourav Ganguly after Rajasthan Royals' takeover buzz IPL 2026: Sourav Ganguly says the IPL now rivals global giants like the EPL and NBA, as soaring valuations and rising global investment underline its rapid growth and commercial dominance.

After quitting PSL, Australia pacer joins CSK as Nathan Ellis's replacement IPL 2026: Australia's Spencer Johnson became the third player to quit Pakistan Super League and join the IPL as a replacement player. Chennai Super Kings signed the left-arm fast bowler for Rs 1.5 crore.

Lakshya Sen, PV Sindhu to lead India's Thomas and Uber Cup campaigns in May While Lakshya Sen will lead India's campaign in Thomas Cup, two-time Olympic medallist PV Sindhu will return to action to lead the Uber Cup team in Horsens, Denmark, from April 24 to May 3.

Bengaluru stampede: What has RCB done to make Chinnaswamy safer for IPL 2026? IPL 2026: RCB have invested Rs 7 crore to overhaul crowd safety at Chinnaswamy Stadium, nearly a year after 11 fans died in a stampede during the team's trophy celebrations.

Rajasthan Royals sold for Rs 15,000 crore to US-based entrepreneur: ReportA US-led consortium has reportedly acquired Rajasthan Royals for USD 1.63 billion, setting a new benchmark in the Indian Premier League. Meanwhile, bidding for RCB intensifies, with valuations expected to cross USD 2 billion following their historic 2025 title and massive global demand.

Klopp rubbishes Real links, leaves door open for return to football managementFormer Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has strongly dismissed repeated speculation linking him with the managerial position at Real Madrid, labelling the reports as complete "nonsense" invented by "idiots".

BCCI bans match-day practice, announces stricter rules in updated guidelinesIPL 2026: The Board of Control for Cricket in India (BCCI) has expanded its operational guidelines for this season, introducing stricter regulations, including a complete ban on match-day practice sessions, according to a report by Cricbuzz.

Smriti Mandhana retains 2nd spot, Harmanpreet rises to 14th in ICC T20I rankingsIndia batter Smriti Mandhana retained her second spot, while skipper Harmanpreet Kaur moved up to 14th in the latest ICC women's T20I rankings for batters on Tuesday.

RCB to keep 11 seats reserved in all matches as tribute to stampede victimsFollowing a tragic stampede and the loss of hosting rights, RCB returns to M Chinnaswamy Stadium with a permanent 11-seat tribute and AI-driven safety protocols for the 2026 IPL season.

Rape accused Yash Dayal will not join RCB in IPL 2026IPL 2026: Rape accused Yash Dayal, who plays for the Royal Challengers Bengaluru, will not join the team this season, confirmed RCB's director of cricket, Mo Babat. Dayal was recently denied bail by a special POSCO court in Jaipur.

IPL 2026: Matthew Hayden picks GT star as heir to his iconic Mongoose batIPL 2026: Gujarat Titans batting coach Matthew Hayden named Rahul Tewatia as the successor to his iconic Mongoose bat, praising the GT finisher's power-hitting ahead of IPL 2026.

KKR's injury crisis deepens, Akash Deep ruled out for the seasonIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders have been hit with yet another injury to a fast bowler. India pacer Akash Deep has been ruled out due to a lumbar stress injury.

Is this the make or break season for Hardik Pandya, the MI captain?IPL: Hardik Pandya's return to Mumbai Indians has not borne the results that it was supposed to. Can Hardik get out of Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav's shadow this season.

If Maradona was God at Napoli, then McTominay is Jesus: Argentine great's sonDiego Maradona Jr sparked debate after declaring that if Diego Maradona was "God" at Napoli, then Scott McTominay is "Jesus", hailing the Scotland midfielder as the club's second-greatest player ever following his sensational rise in Serie A.

The Solidarity of the HopefulAt Patiala's National Institute of Sports, far from the glare of packed stadiums, India's throwers chase excellence in near anonymity. Here, success is forged not in moments of applause but in thousands of unseen repetitions, driven by a stubborn belief that today's effort will echo on a distant global stage.

Miami Open: Sinner breaks Novak Djokovic's record to script ATP Tour historyJannik Sinner created ATP Masters 1000 history on Monday evening at the Miami Open, registering a commanding 6-1, 6-4 win over Corentin Moutet at the Hard Rock Stadium.

Anushka Sharma earns maiden call-up as India announce squad for South Africa T20IsAnushka Sharma earned her first India call-up as the BCCI named the squad for the women's T20I series against South Africa starting April 17. Harmanpreet Kaur continued to lead the side, with Smriti Mandhana serving as her deputy.

Krunal Pandya sets sights on more IPL success with RCB: Just the beginningRCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya was instrumental in the franchise's maiden IPL triumph in 2025, delivering a string of match-winning performances. Yet, the seasoned campaigner remains hungry for more and has already set his sights on further glory.

Rinku Singh set to be appointed regional sports officer by UP governmentIPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rinku Singh is set to be named as the Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh government. Rinku is being rewarded for his exceptional service with the Indian cricket team.

Ben Stokes breaks silence on Ashes drubbing: Hardest period of my captaincyEngland captain Ben Stokes has broken his silence after the team's humiliation at the hands of Australia in Ashes 2025-26. Stokes has called the series the hardest period of his captaincy journey.

MS Dhoni should be the CSK captain in IPL 2026: Aakash ChopraIPL 2026: Aakash Chopra said MS Dhoni should captain CSK if he plays IPL 2026. His comments reignited debate over Dhoni's role in the team.

AIFF women's chief seeks answers after Asian Cup blunders, kit row eruptsIPL 2026: Valanka Alemao demanded accountability after multiple lapses hit India's Asian Cup campaign. The controversy included ill-fitting jerseys and poor planning.

IPL 2026: KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane warns critics with rapid 58 in intra-squad clashIPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane hit a rapid 58 in KKR's intra-squad clash. The knock came amid criticism over his captaincy ahead of IPL 2026.

Bumrah's words, painful lessons: Mayank Yadav's road to recovery for IPL 2026From skipping recovery sessions to surgery and back, Mayank Yadav opens up on his painful road to IPL 2026 and the conversations with Bumrah that kept him going.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
